verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (Hsp70-1/Hsp72) is a canonically cytosolic/nuclear stress-inducible chaperone with no TM domain, but is well-documented to be displayed on the outer leaflet of tumor cells across many entities (breast, lung, colon, pancreatic, glioma, melanoma, hematologic) and not on corresponding normal cells. The membrane form is detected by the cmHsp70.1 mAb (TKD epitope, aa 450-463 in the SBD) on viable tumor cells by flow cytometry and surface iodination, and is being pursued as a tumor-selective therapeutic target (ADCC, ADC-like, radioligand, nanoparticle). Classic cancer ecto-chaperone biology.
